rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Undertaking Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant H&C Investment Associates, Inc.¿s motion for undertaking of costs of $25,000 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Duro Dyne Corporations Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate The Trial To Try Statute Of Limitations
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Washington Group International Inc.¿s joinder to Duro Dyne Corp.¿s motion to bifurcate trial to try statute of limitations is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. 1005(b), C.C.P. 1010.6 and CRC 2.251. Plaintiff¿s objection is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate The Trial To Try Statute Of Limitations
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Duro Dyne Corp.¿s motion to bifurcate trial to try statute of limitations is denied without prejudice. The motion may be raised before the trial judge. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Defendant Cbs Corporation'S (Fka Viacom Inc., Fka Westinghouse Electric Corporation) Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 24, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant CBS Corp.¿s memorandum of costs is continued to October 10, 2013 per agreement of the parties. = (503/TLJ)
